params.content:
# They're vibe-coding spam now

Captured on: 2026-03-23  
HN URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47482760  
Source URL: https://tedium.co/2026/02/25/vibe-coded-email-spam/

Raw text:

The article says spam now looks more visually competent because low-friction AI tools raise the baseline for clean, conventional design. The practical risk is that people lose older visual heuristics for spotting fake messages. The piece points to a lower skill barrier for scam and phishing operations and suggests that generic AI-generated design patterns may become a trust smell over time.

The HN thread adds a few angles. Some people say the deeper issue is weak incentives in free email systems, not AI alone. Others point out that attackers can copy real branded emails instead of generating new ones, which means the problem is not only generation quality but also distribution and filtering.

# They're vibe-coding spam now

- NEW: Captured from HN discussion on 2026-03-23, discussing the impact of AI on spam design and the implications for email trust.
